title: FG commutator identities
summary: Exposes the Q–X–Δ̃ identities already contained in NormalLaplacianModel under names used by the tangentiality calculation.

mathematicalContent.heading: Ambient commutator package
mathematicalContent.body: The four declarations restate the certified model fields in mathematical notation: Q has weight two, Δ̃ lowers weight by two, and its commutator with multiplication by Q has the standard sl₂ form. The Q^m formula is stated for an X-homogeneous function.

mathematicalContent.heading: Proof status
mathematicalContent.body: Each Lean proof is a projection from NormalLaplacianModel. Consequently, Lean verifies that the named statements follow from the model, but this module does not derive those identities from the normal-form metric components.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Within a NormalLaplacianModel, the defining function Q is X-homogeneous of degree two.
correspondence.leanType: theorem straightForm_X_Q (L : NormalLaplacianModel A) : L.X (normalQ A) = fun U => 2 * normalQ A U

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The Euler operator and formal ambient Laplacian satisfy the weight-lowering commutator identity supplied by the model.
correspondence.leanType: theorem straightForm_X_laplacian_comm (L : NormalLaplacianModel A) (f : Operators.Function Conf) : L.X (L.laplacian f) = fun U => L.laplacian (L.X f) U - 2 * L.laplacian f U

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The formal Laplacian of Qf separates into QΔ̃f and the Euler–dimension correction term.
correspondence.leanType: theorem straightForm_lap_Q_comm (L : NormalLaplacianModel A) (f : Operators.Function Conf) : L.laplacian (normalQmul A f) = fun U => normalQ A U * L.laplacian f U + 2 * (2 * L.X f U + (L.baseDim + 2) * f U)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For an X-homogeneous function of weight w, the model supplies the closed formula for one Laplacian applied to Q^m f.
correspondence.leanType: theorem straightForm_lap_Qpow_of_XHomogeneous (L : NormalLaplacianModel A) (m : ℕ) {w : ℝ} {f : Operators.Function Conf} (hf : L.X f = fun U => w * f U) : L.laplacian (normalQpowMul A m f) = fun U => normalQ A U ^ m * L.laplacian f U + 2 * (m : ℝ) * (2 * w + L.baseDim + 2 * (m : ℝ)) * normalQ A U ^ (m - 1) * f U

references.note: Source for the Euler, defining-function, and Laplacian formulas.

limitations: All four results are verified projections of NormalLaplacianModel fields. They are not independent derivations from the metric, Christoffel symbols, or a constructed ambient Laplacian.
